holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for INTC, and the name just gave back ~4% on the day and ~4.6% on the week with the stock at $127.68 — that's a momentum break without a known event to anchor the next window. With nothing scheduled until macro triple witching ~82 days out, there's no asymmetric setup to lean into here; the job is to wait for either an earnings date confirmation or a price level that defines the bounce/break.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro, no INTC-specific catalyst on calendar)
INTC.bull: If selling exhausts near recent support and an earnings date drops, weakness becomes a defined add-on-weakness window.
INTC.bear: Down 4.6% on the week with no catalyst in sight means the tape, not a print, sets the next leg — drift risk dominates.

NVDA.read: NVDA is off 8.75% on the week and -2% today with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window provided. That makes this a price-action read, not an event read: an 8-handle weekly drawdown in the mega-cap AI bellwether is the kind of flush that either resolves into a reclaim of the prior range or extends as positioning unwinds, and without a dated catalyst there's no forced hand here. Triple witching on 2026-09-18 is the only thing on the calendar and it's 82 days out — too far to anchor a window.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (no company-specific catalyst on file)
NVDA.bull: Quality leader washing out 8%+ on no name-specific news is the kind of weakness long-term holders typically get paid for adding into.
NVDA.bear: No dated catalyst means no defined floor; if this is the front of an AI-capex de-rating, the drawdown can extend well past today's print.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 19% over five sessions with no dated company catalyst on the calendar — that's a momentum break without a near-term event to reset the narrative. With no earnings or ex-div date in hand, there's no defined window to lean into; the only thing on the horizon is Sept 18 triple witching, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. The actionable window opens when the next earnings date gets confirmed or when price stops making lower lows on volume.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, no company catalyst on calendar)
ORCL.bull: AI/OCI capex story intact; a 19% five-day drawdown in a quality name often sets up a mean-revert bounce once selling exhausts.
ORCL.bear: No dated catalyst to stop the bleed, and -2.6% today says the de-rating isn't done — knife-catching without an event is just hope.

NOK.read: NOK just dropped -6.84% in a single session with no dated company catalyst on the board, which means today's move is news- or flow-driven rather than event-driven — you're flying without a scheduled print to anchor a window. With nothing closer than triple witching ~82 days out, there's no clean catalyst-defined add or trim setup; the right move is to wait for either a dated Q3 earnings confirmation or a stabilization candle before committing either way.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (no company catalyst on calendar)
NOK.bull: Quality networking name flushing on no scheduled event often sets up a mean-reversion entry once sellers exhaust.
NOK.bear: A -6.84% gap with no catalyst usually means something is leaking — guidance risk or a customer issue — and you're catching it blind.

NBIS.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the sheet for NBIS — no confirmed earnings date, no ex-div, no investor day in the feed. With only triple witching on 2026-09-18 as a macro marker ~82 days out, there's no event-driven window to lean into here; this is a position-sizing and tape question, not a catalyst question. The actionable next step is sourcing a confirmed next earnings date or capacity/customer announcement before framing an add or trim.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NBIS-specific dated catalyst provided)
NBIS.bull: AI infra/neocloud demand and capacity build-out keep the narrative bid; any confirmed hyperscaler-style contract or capacity update is the asymmetric upside.
NBIS.bear: High-beta AI infra name with no near-term dated catalyst to anchor to — vulnerable to factor unwinds and headline-driven gaps, amplified into a quad-witch tape.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position is up 2.7% on the day and 4.1% on the week with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the provided calendar. Without an event to anchor an add or trim window, the recent pop is just tape; the next real decision point will be the next earnings print or ex-div declaration, neither of which is currently dated here.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific catalyst on calendar)
KO.bull: Defensive staple bid is alive — 4.1% in five days on a low-beta name suggests rotation into quality/yield.
KO.bear: No dated catalyst means the move is flow-driven and can unwind just as quickly once rotation fades.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a fresh June 2026 IPO with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — aggregator coverage typically lags new listings by a quarter or two. With no dated company catalyst on the board, the only scheduled event in view is Sept 18 triple witching, which is a liquidity/vol event, not a SpaceX-specific one. Best posture is to let the first post-IPO print get dated before committing to an add/trim window.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro liquidity event; no SpaceX-specific date confirmed yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity name, recently public, any first-earnings date confirmation could pull forward positioning flows.
SPCX.bear: Lock-up overhangs, no confirmed financial calendar, and triple-witching vol can amplify thin post-IPO tape.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
